market_overview.headline: Markets are in broad bearish EMA structure with 33.7% of stocks in Full Bear and only 20.7% in Full Bull, signaling risk-off environment.
market_overview.detail: SPY and QQQ both show bearish unstacked EMAs with potential bullish crosses brewing, while BTC sits in Full Bear structure down 17% from 21W EMA. Energy minerals (100% bullish) and industrial services lead while tech services and finance lag heavily.

reversal_candidates.headline: Early Reversal Signals - Bear Rallies with Bullish Cross Potential

items.crossover_detail: 8W just below 13W (0.72%) — bullish cross potential; 13W just above 21W (0.35%) — bearish cross risk
items.note: Mega-cap AI leader showing early bullish cross setup despite bearish structure - key reversal signal for tech sector.

momentum_themes.headline: Electronic Technology Leading Momentum Surge
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology dominates momentum leaders with memory/semiconductor revival driving gains. Storage (SNDK +58.8%, WDC +32.6%) and optical networking (LITE +56.3%, CIEN +46.5%) showing explosive acceleration.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominance vs Tech/Finance Weakness

strongest.note: Perfect bullish structure with all 15 stocks in Full Bull - commodity supercycle thesis playing out.
Infrastructure and oilfield services benefiting from capex cycle and energy transition investments.
Power generation and grid modernization themes supporting defensive utility strength.

weakest.note: Cloud and software stocks under severe pressure - 85% bearish structure signals major sector rotation.
Banking and financial services hammered by rate cut expectations and credit concerns.
Auto sector completely bearish - consumer spending weakness and EV transition headwinds.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from growth/tech into energy and industrials reflects macro shift toward value and commodity exposure. Financial sector weakness suggests credit cycle concerns despite potential rate relief.

items.text: Both SPY and QQQ in bearish EMA structure with potential bullish crosses - failed breakouts could trigger deeper selling
High volume pullbacks in defensive sectors (LMT, JNJ) may signal institutional distribution rather than healthy retracement
BTC Full Bear structure down 17% from 21W EMA suggests risk-off environment may persist despite equity stabilization attempts
Technology services 85% bearish with major names (MSFT, CRM, ORCL) deeply oversold - sector may need longer base-building period
